Title: Successfully add a new user with valid data
Preconditions: 1. Server is running on port 5000 | 2. Frontend is running on port 3000 | 3. Database connection is established | 4. user_management table exists
Steps: 1. Navigate to /users route | 2. Enter "John Doe" in the Name field | 3. Select "1990-01-15" in Date of Birth field | 4. Enter "123 Main Street, New York, NY 10001" in Address field | 5. Click "Add User" button | 6. Observe success message | 7. Check users table for new entry
Test Data: Name: John Doe | DOB: 1990-01-15 | Address: 123 Main Street, New York, NY 10001
Expected Result: User is successfully added to database | Success message "User added successfully!" appears | Form is cleared | New user appears in the users table below with correct data | Table shows Name, DOB (formatted), Address, Created At, and Delete button

Title: View all users in the table
Preconditions: 1. At least 3 users exist in database | 2. User is on /users page
Steps: 1. Navigate to /users page | 2. Wait for page load | 3. Scroll to "All Users" section | 4. Observe table contents
Test Data: Existing users: User A (DOB: 1985-03-20, Address: 456 Oak Ave), User B (DOB: 1992-07-10, Address: 789 Pine St), User C (DOB: 1988-11-05, Address: 321 Elm Rd)
Expected Result: All users table displays with 5 columns: Name, Date of Birth, Address, Created At, Actions | All 3 users are visible in table | Dates are formatted as MM/DD/YYYY | Delete button is present for each user | Table styling uses Oktawave color scheme (#2DB5DA for headers)

Title: Delete an existing user
Preconditions: 1. At least one user exists in database | 2. User is on /users page | 3. Users table is visible
Steps: 1. Locate a user in the table | 2. Click the "Delete" button for that user | 3. Confirm deletion in browser confirmation dialog | 4. Wait for success message | 5. Observe table update
Test Data: User to delete: "Test User" with any valid DOB and address
Expected Result: Browser confirmation dialog appears with message "Are you sure you want to delete this user?" | After confirming, success message "User deleted successfully!" appears | User is removed from the table | Table refreshes to show remaining users | If it was the last user, "No users found" message displays

Title: Complete user management workflow from landing page
Preconditions: 1. Application is fully running (frontend + backend) | 2. User starts on landing page (/) | 3. Database is empty or has some users
Steps: 1. From landing page, click "Users" link in navigation | 2. Verify /users page loads | 3. Enter name "Sarah Johnson" | 4. Enter DOB "1995-06-20" | 5. Enter address "555 Broadway Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90001" | 6. Click "Add User" button | 7. Verify user appears in table | 8. Click "Back" button | 9. Verify navigation to home page (/) | 10. Return to /users via navigation | 11. Delete the created user | 12. Verify deletion
Test Data: User data: Sarah Johnson, 1995-06-20, 555 Broadway Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90001
Expected Result: Navigation link to Users works from landing page | /users page renders with form and table | User is successfully added after form submission | Success message displays | User appears in table immediately | Back button navigates to landing page | Users link remains functional | User can be deleted successfully | Complete workflow executes without errors

Title: Access user management from all pages with navigation links
Preconditions: 1. Application running | 2. Database has at least 1 user
Steps: 1. Navigate to landing page (/) | 2. Click "Users" link in top nav | 3. Verify /users loads | 4. Navigate to /form (customer form) | 5. Click "Users" button | 6. Verify /users loads | 7. Navigate to /kyc page | 8. Click "Users" button | 9. Verify /users loads | 10. From /users, click "Back" to return to home
Test Data: N/A - Navigation testing only
Expected Result: Users navigation link is present and styled with Oktawave primary color (#2DB5DA) on all pages: /, /form, /kyc | Clicking Users link from any page navigates to /users successfully | Active state shows on /users when on that page | No broken links | Navigation is smooth without errors | All existing routes (/, /form, /kyc) remain functional

Title: Submit form with name containing hyphens and apostrophes
Preconditions: 1. User is on /users page | 2. Form is empty
Steps: 1. Enter "Mary-Jane O'Connor" in Name field | 2. Enter valid DOB "1987-09-12" | 3. Enter valid address "100 Test St" | 4. Click "Add User" button | 5. Check for errors or success
Test Data: Name: Mary-Jane O'Connor | DOB: 1987-09-12 | Address: 100 Test St
Expected Result: Name with hyphens and apostrophes is accepted as valid | No validation error appears | User is successfully created | Name displays correctly in table as "Mary-Jane O'Connor" with special characters intact

Title: Submit form with name containing only spaces
Preconditions: 1. User is on /users page | 2. Form is empty
Steps: 1. Enter "   " (three spaces) in Name field | 2. Enter valid DOB "1990-01-01" | 3. Enter valid address "123 Main St" | 4. Attempt to click "Add User" button | 5. Observe validation
Test Data: Name: three spaces only | DOB: 1990-01-01 | Address: 123 Main St
Expected Result: Validation error appears: "Name is required" | Form submission is blocked | Error message is displayed inline below Name field with red styling | Submit button remains enabled but form does not submit | User remains on page to correct error

Title: Enter user with DOB exactly 18 years ago (boundary case)
Preconditions: 1. User is on /users page | 2. Current date is known
Steps: 1. Calculate date exactly 18 years ago from today | 2. Enter valid name "Test User" | 3. Enter calculated DOB | 4. Enter valid address | 5. Submit form | 6. Check validation result
Test Data: Use date calculation: today minus 18 years | Example: if today is 2024-04-29, use 2006-04-29
Expected Result: User with DOB exactly 18 years ago is accepted (18+ validation passes) | No age validation error | User is successfully created | DOB displays correctly in table

Title: Enter maximum length address (500+ characters)
Preconditions: 1. User is on /users page
Steps: 1. Enter valid name "Long Address User" | 2. Enter valid DOB "1990-05-15" | 3. Enter address with 500+ characters (very long street address with apt, city, state, zip, and additional details) | 4. Submit form | 5. Check result
Test Data: Name: Long Address User | DOB: 1990-05-15 | Address: 500-character string with full details
Expected Result: Long address is accepted without validation error | User is created successfully | Address is stored in database | In table view, address may be truncated or wrapped but full text is preserved | No character limit validation error (minimum 5 chars is the only requirement)

Title: Name field validation accepts valid names with spaces
Preconditions: 1. User is on /users page
Steps: 1. Enter "John Michael Doe" in Name field | 2. Observe inline validation (no error) | 3. Enter valid DOB and address | 4. Submit form
Test Data: Name: John Michael Doe | DOB: 1992-03-10 | Address: 200 Oak Lane
Expected Result: Name with multiple spaces between words is accepted | No validation error | Inline validation does not trigger error | User is created successfully when form is submitted

Title: Address field accepts various address formats
Preconditions: 1. User is on /users page
Steps: 1. Test multiple address formats in sequence: a) "123 Main St", b) "Apt 4B, 456 Oak Ave, Springfield, IL 62701", c) "Building 7, Floor 3, 789 Pine Road" | 2. For each, enter valid name and DOB | 3. Submit each form | 4. Verify all are accepted
Test Data: Various address formats as listed
Expected Result: All address formats meeting minimum 5-character requirement are accepted | No validation errors | Users are created with different address formats | Table displays all addresses correctly

Title: Form shows loading state during submission
Preconditions: 1. User is on /users page | 2. Network may have slight delay
Steps: 1. Fill form with valid data | 2. Click "Add User" button | 3. Immediately observe button state | 4. Wait for submission to complete
Test Data: Name: Quick Test | DOB: 1991-11-11 | Address: 111 Test Ave
Expected Result: Submit button text changes from "Add User" to "Adding User..." | Button becomes disabled during submission | User cannot click button again while submitting | After completion, button returns to enabled state and text returns to "Add User"

Title: Attempt to delete user with cancelled confirmation
Preconditions: 1. At least one user exists in table | 2. User is on /users page
Steps: 1. Locate a user in the table | 2. Click "Delete" button for that user | 3. When browser confirmation dialog appears, click "Cancel" | 4. Observe result
Test Data: Any existing user in table
Expected Result: Browser confirmation dialog appears | When user clicks Cancel, dialog closes | User is NOT deleted from database | User remains in table | No success or error message appears | Table data unchanged

Title: Test API error handling when server is down
Preconditions: 1. Stop the backend server (port 5000) | 2. Frontend is still running | 3. User is on /users page
Steps: 1. With server stopped, fill form with valid data | 2. Click "Add User" button | 3. Wait for response | 4. Observe error handling
Test Data: Name: Test Error | DOB: 1990-01-01 | Address: 123 Main St | Server: stopped
Expected Result: Error message appears: "An error occurred while adding the user. Please try again." | Error displays in red alert box at top of form | Form data is preserved (not cleared) | User can retry after server is restarted | No crash or unhandled exception

Title: Verify all validation errors display simultaneously when submitting completely empty form
Preconditions: 1. User is on /users page | 2. Form is completely empty
Steps: 1. Without entering any data, click "Add User" button | 2. Observe all validation errors
Test Data: All fields: empty
Expected Result: Multiple validation errors appear: "Name is required", "Date of birth is required", "Address is required" | All three errors display simultaneously below their respective fields | General error message at top: "Please fix the errors before submitting." | Form stays on page | No API call is made

Title: Refresh users list after adding multiple users
Preconditions: 1. User is on /users page | 2. Database is empty or has few users
Steps: 1. Add first user: "User One", DOB: 1990-01-01, Address: "100 First St" | 2. Wait for success and table refresh | 3. Add second user: "User Two", DOB: 1991-02-02, Address: "200 Second Ave" | 4. Wait for refresh | 5. Add third user: "User Three", DOB: 1992-03-03, Address: "300 Third Blvd" | 6. Observe final table state
Test Data: Three users with sequential data
Expected Result: After each submission, users table automatically refreshes | All three users appear in table in reverse chronological order (newest first) | Each user has all data visible: Name, DOB, Address, Created At | All delete buttons are functional | No duplicate entries | No page reload required for updates
